title: As France faces demographic decline, immigration emerges as both political and economic necessity
subhead: Immigration alone will not be enough to offset France's negative natural population growth in the medium and long term, according to the latest projections from the national statistics institute.

Macron's surprise victory in 2017 inspires new wave of French presidential hopefuls
subhead: About 30 contenders have already announced their candidacy for the 2027 presidential election or have not ruled out doing so, while the far-right Rassemblement National and the radical left La France Insoumise are already gearing up for the campaign.

title: Israel bars RFI journalist from entering West Bank and increases pressure on foreign press
subhead: The expulsion of Alice Froussard, a journalist for Radio France Internationale (RFI), comes amid severe violence in the occupied territory and a total closure of the Gaza Strip to international media, where more than 260 Palestinian journalists have been killed since the attack of October 7, 2023.

title: Human-caused climate change set to reach 1.5°C around 2030
subhead: In a study published Thursday, a consortium of researchers confirms that the most ambitious threshold of the Paris Agreement on climate change, signed in 2015, is likely to be exceeded within the next four years.

title: Cocaine transporters, 'narcos' before their time, face trial after 15-year investigation
subhead: France is trying one of the country's oldest major cocaine trafficking cases, dating back to 2011 and involving smuggling between Guadeloupe and mainland France. The alleged ringleader has been identified and apprehended in Panama after years on the run.

subhead: Although rooted in a bygone era and battered by Donald Trump, the Group of Seven continues to meet purely out of habit, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris, one week before the summit chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron in Evian.

title: The murky ties between FIFA and the US
subhead: After the 2022 World Cup was awarded to Qatar, the US launched a legal offensive against several FIFA members before securing the co-hosting of the 2026 tournament. This is the story of the rise of American soft power in global football.
